dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S5013 IS: Veteran Acquired Brain Injury Caregiving Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cassidy introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs

form.official-title: To require the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to carry out a pilot program to use amounts under the Veteran-Directed Care program to provide care to veterans with acquired brain injury, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Veteran Acquired Brain Injury Caregiving Act.

section.header: Pilot program on use of amounts under Veteran-Directed Care program to provide care to veterans with acquired brain injury

subsection.text: Beginning not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall carry out a five-year pilot program under which an eligible veteran may use funds under the Veteran-Directed Care program to purchase care from an eligible veteran mission-driven nonprofit.
The Secretary shall select not fewer than five medical centers of the Department of Veterans Affairs at which to carry out the pilot program under subsection (a).

Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, and not less frequently than annually thereafter until the termination of the pilot program under subsection (a), the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs of the Senate and the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs of the House of Representatives a report containing the following:

paragraph.text: The number of eligible veterans receiving assistance under the pilot program.
The number of eligible veteran mission-driven nonprofits participating in the pilot program.
An evaluation of the clinical outcomes of veterans participating in the pilot program, veteran satisfaction with the pilot program, and other relevant data the Secretary determines appropriate.
The term eligible veteran means a veteran who—

subparagraph.text: is clinically assessed to have an acquired brain injury; and
is an eligible veteran under section 1720L of title 38, United States Code.

paragraph.text: The term eligible veteran mission-driven nonprofit means an organization—

subparagraph.text: described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 that is exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of such Code; and
that provides services provided under the Veteran-Directed Care program for a veteran.

paragraph.text: The term Veteran-Directed Care program means the program under section 1720L(b) of title 38, United States Code.
